Economy & Business.tagline: Innovation and growth fueling global prosperity

stories.title: Asian Development Bank Pledges $70 Billion for Clean Energy and Digital Access
stories.summary: ADB's bold new initiative will connect 200 million people to clean power through cross-border renewable grids, and build AI-ready digital highways across Asia-Pacific by 2035 — creating 840,000 jobs and cutting regional power emissions by 15%.

stories.title: Canada and Mexico Forge Stronger Ties With 10+ New Trade Agreements
stories.summary: A Mexican trade mission to Toronto and Montréal resulted in over 10 MOUs spanning life sciences, agrifood, education, and creative industries. Highlights include Grupo Bimbo's CA$200 million investment to modernise its Canadian bakeries, deepening North American resilience.

stories.title: Kenya and France Seal 11 Landmark Deals on Rail, Energy, and Digital
stories.summary: Presidents Ruto and Macron signed 11 sweeping agreements including a KSh 12.5 billion modernisation of Nairobi commuter rail and a KSh 104 billion joint logistics venture. The deals also span renewable energy, cybersecurity, and blue economy cooperation.

Science & Technology.tagline: Pushing the boundaries of human ingenuity

stories.title: India and Japan Unite on Quantum Technology and Health Research
stories.summary: Ministers from both nations signed cooperation agreements in quantum computing, AI, and medical devices in New Delhi, combining Japan's robotics expertise with India's data science capabilities in a frontier-tech partnership set to shape the next wave of innovation.

stories.title: 28 New Deep-Sea Species Found Alongside World's Largest Cold-Water Coral Reef Off Argentina
stories.summary: Scientists aboard the R/V Falkor discovered 28 potential new species — including worms, urchins, and anemones — plus the world's largest known Bathelia candida coral reef, roughly the size of Vatican City, in Argentina's remote deep Atlantic waters.

stories.title: Scientists Transfer Naked Mole Rat Longevity Gene to Mice — They Live Longer
stories.summary: University of Rochester researchers moved a high-molecular-weight hyaluronic acid gene from naked mole rats into mice, extending median lifespan by 4.4%, reducing tumors, and cutting inflammation — proving longevity traits evolved in one species can benefit another.

Environment & Climate.tagline: Restoring the balance of our natural world

stories.title: Prague and Copenhagen Reunited by Direct Train After a Decade Apart
stories.summary: Czech Railways launched daily ComfortJet services connecting Prague to Copenhagen via Berlin and Hamburg on May 1, restoring a link missing for over ten years. The 230 kph trains make the journey in 13 hours and feature restaurant cars, Wi-Fi, and a children's cinema.

stories.title: Colombia's Amazon Deforestation Falls 25% for Second Consecutive Year
stories.summary: Colombia logged one of its lowest deforestation totals since 2016, with primary forest loss dropping 25% in 2025 thanks to Indigenous-led land governance, the Conservar Paga payment program supporting 5,500+ families, and expanded Amazon ecotourism.

stories.title: NASA Confirms Southern Ocean Is a More Powerful Carbon Sink Than We Thought
stories.summary: New aircraft measurements confirm the frigid Southern Ocean absorbs significantly more atmospheric CO₂ than climate models previously estimated, revealing a vital natural buffer against global warming that scientists are now working to better protect and monitor.

Health & Wellness.tagline: Nurturing the mind, body, and spirit

stories.title: WHO Validates Australia as First High-Income Nation to Eliminate Trachoma
stories.summary: Australia has officially eradicated trachoma — the world's leading infectious cause of blindness — becoming the 30th country globally and first wealthy nation to earn WHO validation. Decades of partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities made it possible.

stories.title: Blood Test for Brain Tumors Earns FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation
stories.summary: A simple blood test developed at Imperial College Healthcare NHS Trust that detects brain tumors has been granted FDA Breakthrough Therapy status, fast-tracking its path to clinic and offering hope for earlier, less invasive diagnosis for thousands of patients globally.

stories.title: Semaglutide Delivers Remarkable Weight Loss and Heart Benefits in Adults 65+
stories.summary: Presented at the European Congress on Obesity 2026, a new analysis found semaglutide helped adults over 65 lose an average of 15% of body weight in 68 weeks. Two-thirds achieved over 10% loss, with meaningful improvements in heart health and metabolic markers.

Society & Culture.tagline: The vibrant threads of human connection

stories.title: Africa's First-Ever Road Safety Charter Enters Into Force
stories.summary: After 15 African Union nations ratified it, the continent's historic Road Safety Charter came into force, committing member states to halve road fatalities by 2030. Nigeria has already recorded a 12% drop in road deaths in Q1 2026.

stories.title: Georgia Invests $70 Million to Put Literacy Coaches in Every Elementary School
stories.summary: Governor Kemp signed the Georgia Early Literacy Act of 2026, deploying 1,300 dedicated literacy coaches to all K-3 public schools statewide. The $70 million investment also expands kindergarten access and creates a task force to strengthen early reading outcomes.

stories.title: Marshall Islands New Aircraft Opens a Lifeline to Remote Island Communities
stories.summary: The Marshall Islands welcomed its first Cessna SkyCourier, carrying 5,000 lbs more cargo than the aging Dornier it replaces. President Heine called it a new chapter — ensuring outer-island families can reach medical care, students can travel for education, and essential goods flow.

Entertainment & Arts.tagline: Stories and visions that move the soul

stories.title: 36th Macao Arts Festival Opens Celebrating East-West Cultural Dialogue
stories.summary: The beloved Macao Arts Festival launched its 36th edition on May 8, gathering artists from across Asia and the world for performances, exhibitions, and cross-cultural collaborations that transform the historic UNESCO-listed city into a living stage.

stories.title: New Taipei Multicultural Festival Celebrates World Folk Arts at City Museum
stories.summary: New Taipei City's Multicultural Festival 2026 opened at the New Taipei City Art Museum under the theme 'World Folk Arts Club,' blending live music, handcrafted markets, and cultural performances from dozens of global traditions in a vibrant celebration.

stories.title: Eddie Murphy to Receive AFI Life Achievement Award in Netflix Tribute
stories.summary: Hollywood legend Eddie Murphy will be honored at the AFI Life Achievement Award ceremony, with a star-studded tribute streaming on Netflix on May 31. The recognition celebrates his decades of groundbreaking comedy, film, and lasting cultural impact across generations.

Sports & Leisure.tagline: The joy of movement and the spirit of play

stories.title: PSG Reach Champions League Final Against Arsenal After Beating Bayern
stories.summary: Defending champions Paris Saint-Germain defeated Bayern Munich to set up a glamour Champions League final against Arsenal at Budapest's Puskás Aréna on May 30. The all-or-nothing clash promises one of the most captivating finals in years.

stories.title: Wings for Life World Run Smashes Records: 9.2 Million Euro and 346,527 Runners
stories.summary: The 13th Wings for Life World Run set records in fundraising and participation on May 11, with 346,527 runners from 173 countries raising €9.2 million for spinal cord injury research — pushing the all-time total to €69.7 million in the pursuit of a cure.

stories.title: LeBron James Becomes First Player in NBA History to Play 300 Playoff Games
stories.summary: LeBron James made NBA history on May 7 by appearing in his 300th playoff game, surpassing the previous record by 41 games. At 41, the four-time champion continues to rewrite the postseason record books with the Los Angeles Lakers.

Pets & Wildlife.tagline: Every creature, wild and close to home

stories.title: European Bison Released in Spain's Iberian Highlands for the First Time
stories.summary: Nine European bison were released into Spain's Iberian Highlands, marking a historic first for the species in Iberia. Europe's bison population has surged from just 2,500 to around 9,000 over the past decade, a stunning conservation comeback across the continent.

stories.title: Blight-Resistant American Chestnut Trees Planted at the White House
stories.summary: On Arbor Day, the National Park Service planted blight-tolerant American chestnut trees at the White House — a landmark in efforts to restore a species nearly wiped from 200 million acres of eastern forest by an imported Asian blight a century ago.

stories.title: Stunning New Glass Frog Species Discovered in Ecuador's Cloud Forest Reserve
stories.summary: Scientists identified the Dajomes glassfrog (Nymphargus dajomesae) at Ecuador's El Quimi Nature Reserve, where over 85% of observed amphibians are new to science. The tiny emerald-backed frog joins a growing list of species found nowhere else on Earth.

Economy & Business.tagline: Innovation and growth fueling global prosperity

stories.title: Ghana’s Cedi Ranks Among Africa’s Best-Performing Currencies
stories.summary: Ghana’s cedi emerged as one of Africa’s top-performing currencies in April 2026, cementing the country’s economic recovery following years of IMF-backed fiscal reform. Renewed investor confidence and improved macroeconomic stability are driving the turnaround for one of West Africa’s leading economies.

stories.title: EU Solar Push Could Halve Electricity Costs and Save €223 Billion by 2030
stories.summary: A new SolarPower Europe report found that aggressively scaling solar and battery storage across the EU could cut electricity system costs by nearly half before the decade ends, saving €223 billion annually in fossil fuel imports. Clean energy is now both the fastest and cheapest route to European energy security.

stories.title: Brazil Welcomes Record 9.3 Million Visitors in Q1 2026, Tourism Up 37%
stories.summary: Brazil recorded its best-ever first quarter for international tourism, welcoming 9.3 million visitors—a 37% jump over the previous year. Argentina surpassed the US as Brazil’s top source market, reflecting strengthened regional ties and surging demand for Brazil’s cultural festivals, beaches, and ecotourism experiences.

Science & Technology.tagline: Pushing the boundaries of human ingenuity

stories.title: Scientists Find “Holy Grail” Gene That Lets Axolotls Regrow Limbs
stories.summary: Researchers identified the SP8 transcription factor as a critical driver of limb regeneration in axolotls, and demonstrated it can trigger similar processes in mice, which normally cannot regrow lost tissue. The finding is being hailed as a foundational step toward therapies that could one day help humans recover from traumatic injuries and organ damage.

stories.title: Antarctic Voyage Maps 400 Deep-Sea Species Near Unexplored Cook Ice Shelf
stories.summary: Australia’s CSIRO research vessel RV Investigator completed the COOKIES expedition, documenting over 400 deep-sea species and capturing more than 10,000 images from the rarely visited Cook Ice Shelf marine region. The voyage delivered the most detailed survey of life in these icy depths ever conducted, opening a new window into Antarctic biodiversity.

stories.title: Blue Origin’s Moon Lander Clears Critical NASA Vacuum Chamber Testing
stories.summary: Blue Origin’s Blue Moon MK1 lunar lander successfully completed high-fidelity thermal vacuum testing at NASA’s facility, clearing a major milestone on the path to the Artemis lunar landing programme. The tests validated the lander’s performance in the extreme conditions of space ahead of its planned crewed Moon mission.

Environment & Climate.tagline: Restoring the balance of our natural world

stories.title: Amsterdam Bans Fossil Fuel and Meat Ads From All Public Spaces
stories.summary: Amsterdam became the world’s first capital city to prohibit advertising for fossil fuels and meat products across all public spaces, from bus shelters to city buses and billboards. The policy extends the city’s 2022 fossil fuel ad ban into dietary choices as part of its ambitious climate action plan.

stories.title: South Korean Youth Activist Wins Goldman Environmental Prize for Asia
stories.summary: 31-year-old Borim Kim, founder of Youth 4 Climate Action, won the 2026 Goldman Environmental Prize for leading the first successful youth climate lawsuit in Asia. Her campaign resulted in South Korea’s Constitutional Court ruling that inadequate climate policies violated citizens’ constitutional rights, mandating binding emissions targets through 2049.

stories.title: Pacific Resilience Facility Enters Into Force as Fiji Completes Ratification
stories.summary: The Pacific Resilience Facility Treaty officially came into force on May 6 after Fiji and Australia deposited ratification instruments in Suva. Described as the first Pacific-led climate financing facility, it provides grant funding for community resilience projects, clean energy, and climate adaptation across island nations, with a target of $1.5 billion.

Health & Wellness.tagline: Nurturing the mind, body, and spirit

stories.title: Africa Launches Decade-Long Health Workforce Agenda in Accra
stories.summary: Health ministers and heads of state gathered in Accra for the 2nd Africa Health Workforce Investment Forum, adopting the 2026–2035 "Plan. Train. Retain." roadmap. Backed by a $4.4 billion World Bank portfolio, the agenda targets creating over 2 million healthcare jobs and closing critical staffing gaps across the continent.

stories.title: Mexico Launches Universal Healthcare for All 120 Million Residents
stories.summary: President Claudia Sheinbaum signed a historic decree establishing Mexico’s Universal Health Service, committing to free, comprehensive healthcare for every resident by 2027. The programme prioritises underserved rural and Indigenous communities who have historically had the least access to care.

stories.title: Shingles Vaccine Cuts Heart Attack and Stroke Risk by Nearly Half in Study
stories.summary: A major study presented at the American College of Cardiology found that the shingles vaccine reduces the risk of heart attack and stroke by nearly 50% in patients with existing cardiovascular disease. The finding adds a powerful new reason to vaccinate, with implications for millions of older adults worldwide.

Society & Culture.tagline: The vibrant threads of human connection

stories.title: Niue Elects Record Seven Women to Parliament, Crossing UN Gender Threshold
stories.summary: The tiny Pacific island of Niue made history by electing seven women to its 20-seat parliament, achieving 35% female representation and crossing the UN’s “critical mass” threshold for gender equity in governance. Female representation in Pacific parliaments typically sits below 10%, making Niue’s result a landmark for the region.

stories.title: Lula and Trump Declare Brazil-US Relations “Stabilized” After White House Summit
stories.summary: Brazilian President Lula and President Trump emerged from a three-hour White House meeting with an agreement to resolve trade tensions within 30 days. The breakthrough—linked to US interest in Brazil’s vast critical minerals reserves—signals a new chapter for hemispheric economic cooperation.

stories.title: 1,500 Beagles Freed From Wisconsin Research Facility in Historic Rescue
stories.summary: More than 1,500 beagles began leaving the Ridglan Farms research facility in Wisconsin after animal rescue organisations struck a landmark deal for their release. Transported to shelters and foster homes across the US, the dogs—many bred for laboratory use—are receiving care and being matched with adoptive families.

Entertainment & Arts.tagline: Stories and visions that move the soul

stories.title: Filipino Girl Group BINI Wins Billboard Global Force Award
stories.summary: Eight-member Filipino group BINI made history as the first Southeast Asian act to receive the Billboard Global Force Award, presented at the Hollywood Palladium. Performing in traditional Filipino attire, they dedicated the honour to their fans and declared themselves “proud Filipinas” on the world stage.

stories.title: Bangkok Confirmed as Host of Inaugural Eurovision Song Contest Asia
stories.summary: Bangkok, Thailand was officially named host city for the first-ever Eurovision Song Contest Asia, launching in November 2026. The European Broadcasting Union’s expansion brings the beloved competition format to Asia-Pacific for the first time, with 18 participating countries from across the region.

stories.title: AECFest 2026 Opens With 11 Productions Across 19 Countries in Utrecht
stories.summary: The Asia-Europe Cultural Festival launched its most ambitious programme yet, bringing 11 original productions from 19 countries to Utrecht and Singapore in a two-week celebration of cross-cultural collaboration. The festival bridges Asian and European artists in theatre, dance, and visual arts, running 14–23 May 2026.

Sports & Leisure.tagline: The joy of movement and the spirit of play

stories.title: FIFA Votes to Recognise Afghanistan Women’s Football Team, Defying Taliban
stories.summary: FIFA officially recognised the Afghanistan Women’s National Football Team, allowing players in exile to compete internationally despite the Taliban’s blanket ban on women’s sport at home. The historic vote opens a path toward Olympic qualification and offers Afghan women athletes a lifeline to continue playing at the highest level.

stories.title: UEFA Futsal Champions League Final Brings the Beautiful Game to Pesaro
stories.summary: Sporting CP and four-time champions Palma met in a sold-out Pesaro Futsal Arena for the UEFA Futsal Champions League Final, the pinnacle of European club futsal. The Final Four weekend in Italy showcased spectacular semi-final comebacks with both matches decided on penalties after full-time draws.

stories.title: 20-Year-Old Croatian Dino Prižmić Stuns Djokovic at the Italian Open
stories.summary: 20-year-old Dino Prižmić defeated Novak Djokovic 2-6, 6-2, 6-4 in the second round of the Italian Open in Rome, delivering the biggest win of the young Croatian’s career. Coming from a set down, Prižmić outplayed his childhood idol to record his second top-10 win of the season on clay.

Pets & Wildlife.tagline: Every creature, wild and close to home

stories.title: Kenya Opens World’s Largest Black Rhino Sanctuary in Tsavo West
stories.summary: Kenya officially opened a 3,200 square kilometre sanctuary in Tsavo West National Park, protecting 200 black rhinos in the largest concentration of the species in the country. President Ruto called the milestone a symbol of Kenya’s commitment to doubling its national rhino population by 2037.

stories.title: UK Common Cranes Soar to Record Numbers in Historic Breeding Season
stories.summary: Britain’s population of common cranes reached an all-time high in 2026, with the iconic birds now breeding successfully across wetlands in East Anglia and beyond. The milestone reflects decades of habitat restoration work and ranks as one of the UK’s most celebrated wildlife comeback stories.

stories.title: Endangered Right Whales Celebrate Baby Boom With 23 Calves This Season
stories.summary: Scientists documented 23 North Atlantic right whale calves this season—double last year’s count and the highest tally in 17 years for one of the world’s most endangered marine mammals. The baby boom brings cautious hope for a species with only around 380 individuals remaining.

Economy & Business.tagline: Innovation and growth fueling global prosperity

stories.title: US Job Growth Nearly Doubles Forecasts With 115,000 April Additions
stories.summary: The US economy added 115,000 jobs in April 2026, nearly double the analyst forecast of 65,000, while unemployment held steady at 4.3%. The stronger-than-expected result signals continued labor-market resilience despite global trade uncertainty, offering a lift to workers and businesses across North America.

stories.title: Brazil Positions Itself as Critical Minerals Leader With New Strategy
stories.summary: Brazil's lower chamber voted to create a National Critical Minerals Strategy, establishing incentives for extraction and processing of the country's vast reserves of lithium, cobalt, and rare earths. The move positions Brazil as a cornerstone supplier for the global clean-energy transition, turning natural wealth into long-term economic opportunity.

stories.title: Latin America's Economy Set to Grow 2.1% in 2026 Despite Headwinds
stories.summary: Despite global trade disruptions, Latin America is on course for 2.1% GDP growth in 2026, building on a resilient prior year. Analysts credit diversified export markets and deepening regional trade links for sustaining momentum — a signal of growing economic strength across the continent.

Science & Technology.tagline: Pushing the boundaries of human ingenuity

stories.title: BOE and UNESCO Launch Asia-Pacific Science Club Network
stories.summary: BOE Technology and UNESCO unveiled a new Asia-Pacific Science Club Network and 2026 Science Challenge, uniting 40 Chinese teachers with 15 educators from 12 Asia-Pacific countries. Launched under the UN's International Decade of Sciences for Sustainable Development, it will connect young scientists across the region for collaborative hands-on projects.

stories.title: Inside Coral Reefs: 645 New Microbial Species Mapped in Pacific Survey
stories.summary: Researchers from the University of Galway reconstructed genomes of 645 microbial species from 99 Pacific coral reefs, finding over 99% had never been genetically described. Published in Nature on May 8, the study reveals a vast pharmaceutical library hidden inside coral ecosystems — one that could yield future life-saving medicines.

stories.title: Scientists Find Earth's Oldest Air in 6-Million-Year-Old Antarctic Ice
stories.summary: Researchers from the Center for Oldest Ice Exploration discovered 6-million-year-old ice in East Antarctica's Allan Hills, more than doubling the previous record for the oldest dated ice. Tiny air bubbles sealed within offer a direct window into Earth's ancient atmosphere, yielding the first direct measurements of how much Antarctica has cooled over millions of years.

Environment & Climate.tagline: Restoring the balance of our natural world

stories.title: Canon and Oceans Alive Restore Coral Reefs Along Kenya's Coast
stories.summary: Canon Central and North Africa has partnered with Kenya's Oceans Alive Foundation to scale up coral nursery propagation and women-led reef rehabilitation along the country's northern coastline. The initiative builds on a decade of work that already recorded a 300% rise in fish biomass within protected zones.

stories.title: Thames Reborn: River Now Supports 115 Fish Species After Decades of Recovery
stories.summary: The tidal Thames now hosts over 115 fish species, 92 bird species, and nearly 600 hectares of saltmarsh — a stunning turnaround for a river declared biologically dead in 1957. The Zoological Society of London's annual health check confirms that seals, seahorses, and even sharks are now regular residents.

stories.title: Brazil's Cattle Ranchers Become Unexpected Heroes of Pantanal Conservation
stories.summary: Ranchers in Brazil's Pantanal — the world's largest tropical wetland — are partnering with Pew Charitable Trusts conservationists to protect biodiversity alongside their herds. The unlikely alliance is showing that sustainable ranching and thriving wildlife can coexist, offering a replicable model for conservation across agricultural landscapes.

Health & Wellness.tagline: Nurturing the mind, body, and spirit

stories.title: West Africa Launches Bold Roadmap for Universal Health Coverage
stories.summary: Ministers of health and finance from 24 Western and Central African countries gathered in Accra to adopt the World Bank's Fit to Prosper health strategy. The initiative targets universal health coverage for 200 million people by 2030, backed by sustained regional investment and strengthened community health systems.

stories.title: France Bans Forever Chemicals From Cosmetics and Clothing
stories.summary: France became the world's first country to ban PFAS from cosmetics, clothing, and ski wax where alternatives exist, with restrictions now fully in force. The law also mandates drinking-water testing for PFAS contamination and fines industrial polluters — setting a bold new global standard for protecting human health.

stories.title: Inside Coral Reefs: 645 New Microbial Species Mapped in Pacific Survey
stories.summary: Researchers from the University of Galway reconstructed genomes of 645 microbial species from 99 Pacific coral reefs, finding over 99% had never been genetically described. Published in Nature on May 8, the study reveals a vast pharmaceutical library hidden inside coral ecosystems — one that could yield future life-saving medicines.

Society & Culture.tagline: The vibrant threads of human connection

stories.title: Japan Becomes First G7 Nation to Allow Joint Child Custody
stories.summary: Japan's revised Civil Code took effect April 1, allowing divorced parents to negotiate shared parental authority for the first time in the country's history. The landmark reform ends Japan's status as the only G7 nation with mandatory sole custody, and introduces a statutory minimum child-support system.

stories.title: NBA Pioneer Jason Collins Wins Inaugural Bill Walton Champion Award
stories.summary: Jason Collins — who made history in 2014 as the first openly gay active athlete in any major US professional sports league — received the inaugural Bill Walton Global Champion Award at the Green Sports Alliance Summit on May 6. The award honors athletes who use their platform to drive positive social and environmental change.

stories.title: Brazil's Cattle Ranchers Become Unexpected Heroes of Pantanal Conservation
stories.summary: Ranchers in Brazil's Pantanal — the world's largest tropical wetland — are partnering with Pew Charitable Trusts conservationists to protect biodiversity alongside their herds. The unlikely alliance is showing that sustainable ranching and thriving wildlife can coexist, offering a replicable model for conservation across agricultural landscapes.

Entertainment & Arts.tagline: Stories and visions that move the soul

stories.title: The World Celebrates David Attenborough's Remarkable 100th Birthday
stories.summary: Sir David Attenborough turned 100 on May 8, drawing tributes from King Charles III, Leonardo DiCaprio, and scientists who named a new wasp genus in his honor. LEGO updated its age range to 4-100+, post boxes across the UK were adorned with crocheted wildlife, and the Royal Albert Hall hosted a centenary concert.

stories.title: South Korea's Baeksang Awards Break Ground With New Musical Division
stories.summary: The 62nd Baeksang Arts Awards made history on May 8 by establishing a new Musical Division in partnership with the Korea Musical Theatre Association, marking 60 years of Korean musical theatre. Hyun Bin and Park Bo Young took top honors, while the ceremony drew global audiences celebrating the depth of Korean storytelling.

stories.title: Peabody Awards Honor Documentaries on Sly Stone and Fela Kuti
stories.summary: The 86th Peabody Awards celebrated outstanding storytelling, with programs about music legends Sly Stone and Fela Kuti among the honored works. Special awards recognized Amy Poehler's career achievement, screenwriter Sterlin Harjo as a trailblazer, and PBS KIDS as a historic institution — honoring media that uplifts and connects communities.

Sports & Leisure.tagline: The joy of movement and the spirit of play

stories.title: NBA Pioneer Jason Collins Wins Inaugural Bill Walton Champion Award
stories.summary: Jason Collins — who made history in 2014 as the first openly gay active athlete in any major US professional sports league — received the inaugural Bill Walton Global Champion Award at the Green Sports Alliance Summit on May 6. The award honors athletes who use their platform to drive positive social and environmental change.

stories.title: New Zealand Archers Sweep All Medals at Home Oceania Championships
stories.summary: New Zealand's archers delivered a dominant performance on home soil at the 2026 World Archery Oceania Target Championships in Auckland, sweeping the recurve individual podiums in both the men's and women's events. The clean-sweep victory lit up home crowds and reflected years of sustained investment in the sport.

stories.title: Alcaraz, Sabalenka and McIlroy Among 2026 Laureus World Sports Award Winners
stories.summary: The 2026 Laureus World Sports Awards in Madrid celebrated a year of extraordinary sporting achievement, honoring global champions including Carlos Alcaraz, Aryna Sabalenka, and Rory McIlroy. The ceremony also spotlighted Laureus Sport for Good programs using athletics to transform the lives of young people in underserved communities worldwide.

Pets & Wildlife.tagline: Every creature, wild and close to home

stories.title: Second Set of Rare Gorilla Twins Born at Virunga in 2026
stories.summary: For the second time in three months, twin mountain gorillas were born at DRC's Virunga National Park — among the rarest births in the primate world. The Baraka family now numbers 19 individuals, and 9 new gorilla babies have arrived at Virunga in the first three months of 2026 alone.

stories.title: New Zealand's Kākāpō Parrots Hatch 100+ Chicks in Record Breeding Year
stories.summary: New Zealand's critically endangered kākāpō parrots have had their best breeding season on record, with more than 100 chicks hatching across three island sanctuaries. The flightless parrots only breed when rimu trees mass-fruit every few years, making 2026's bumper result a genuine conservation triumph for this beloved species.

stories.title: Kiwi Birds Enter New Zealand Parliament in Historic Conservation Celebration
stories.summary: Kiwi birds were brought into New Zealand's Parliament Banquet Hall in a moving ceremony celebrating the species' remarkable recovery. Rangers described the moment as proof of what's possible when communities work together, with kiwi populations rising steadily thanks to a decade of predator control and community-led protection.
